---
title: "Build a Data Quality Reviewer with Claude"
description: "Discover how to create an app to check data quality to enhance your data analysis and ensure the accuracy of your insights."
author: "Flavio Matos"
date: "June 14, 2026"
categories: [Data Quality, AI, Claude]
event: "NYC AI Session, Online"
image: "cover_image.png"
video-url: https://youtu.be/q3KL24sLzCM?si=sBFaa6pvoUDyp5gd 
format: html
---

### About the event

In this short video for the **NYC AI Learning Sessions** hosted by the [Fund for the City of New York](https://www.fcny.org), I show how to build a simple Data Quality Reviewer using **Claude Artifacts** with a few prompts.

With Claude Artifacts, you can create an app where someone simply uploads a file and gets a quick review of common data quality issues: missing values, incorrect data types, blank rows, and other inconsistencies that often take time to spot manually and also fix small typos and errors.

It's a practical example of how anyone can use AI to automate repetitive tasks and make their day-to-day work more efficient. Building small tools like this can save time, create more consistent outputs, and even help you make better use of your AI credits for more work.

💡 I hope this example inspires you to think about the small tasks in your own workflow that could become simple AI-powered apps.

### Recording

{{< video https://youtu.be/q3KL24sLzCM?si=sBFaa6pvoUDyp5gd >}}
